1:// canvas-engine pattern-library · detection → pattern group normalizer
2:// Maps the open vocabulary of region.detection (r12-cursor-pointer, sem-h3, ...)
3:// to a small set of pattern groups so the registry stays compact.
4://
5:// Two-way contract with tools/anatomy-extension/lib/detection-vocab.js — when
6:// a new detection string is added there, mirror it here (and vice versa).
7:
8:import type { Region } from '../adapter/v1-schema.js';
9:import type { PatternGroup } from './types.js';
10:
11:/**
12: * Distribution from the 7-capture survey (655 regions):
13: *   clickable  ~80%  (r12-cursor-pointer, r7-native-interactive, r8/r9/r11/r5)
14: *   heading    ~10%  (sem-h1...sem-h6)
15: *   list       ~4%   (pattern-repeat, kind=list)
16: *   landmark   ~2.5% (sem-header/section/main/footer/nav/aside)
17: *   card       ~1.4% (card-heuristic, kind=card)
18: *   form       ~0.2% (form)
19: *   default    everything else
20: *
21: * v0.4 vocab additions (extension manual + observation paths):
22: *   auto-label, alt-click, manual, legacy, custom-element, cursor-dwell
23: *   → all routed to "clickable" so existing captures rebucket cleanly.
24: */
25:// Selector leaf-tag is independent ground truth · the cascade lossy-flattens
26:// (r7-native-interactive covers <a>, <button>, <input>, <textarea> all the same)
27:// but the DOM tag in the selector path is browser-emitted and unambiguous.
28:// Trust the tag for the small set whose group is fixed.
29:const LEAF_TAG_RE = /\>\s*([a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9-]*)(?:[:.\[#]|$)/g;
30:
31:function leafTag(selector: string | undefined): string | null {
32:  if (!selector) return null;
33:  let last: string | null = null;
34:  for (const m of selector.matchAll(LEAF_TAG_RE)) last = m[1].toLowerCase();
35:  return last;
36:}
37:
38:const TAG_OVERRIDES: Record<string, PatternGroup> = {
39:  input: 'form',
40:  textarea: 'form',
41:  select: 'form',
42:  ul: 'list',
43:  ol: 'list',
44:};
45:
46:export function normalizeDetection(region: Region): PatternGroup {
47:  const detection = (region.detection || '').toLowerCase();
48:  const kind = (region.kind || '').toLowerCase();
49:  const name = (region.name || '').toLowerCase();
50:
51:  // Tag-based override · the selector path is independent of the cascade.
52:  const tag = leafTag(region.selector);
53:  if (tag && TAG_OVERRIDES[tag]) return TAG_OVERRIDES[tag];
54:
55:  if (kind === 'card' || detection === 'card-heuristic') return 'card';
56:
57:  if (
58:    detection === 'pattern-repeat' ||
59:    kind === 'list'
60:  ) return 'list';
61:
62:  if (
63:    detection === 'form' ||
64:    detection === 'sem-form' ||
65:    name === 'form' ||
66:    name.includes('search bar')
67:  ) return 'form';
68:
69:  if (/^sem-h[1-6]$/.test(detection) || detection === 'heading') return 'heading';
70:
71:  if (
72:    detection === 'sem-header' ||
73:    detection === 'sem-footer' ||
74:    detection === 'sem-nav' ||
75:    detection === 'sem-main' ||
76:    detection === 'sem-aside' ||
77:    detection === 'sem-section' ||
78:    detection === 'landmark'
79:  ) return 'landmark';
80:
81:  if (
82:    detection.startsWith('r2-') ||
83:    detection.startsWith('r3-') ||
84:    detection.startsWith('r4-') ||
85:    detection.startsWith('r5-') ||
86:    detection.startsWith('r7-') ||
87:    detection.startsWith('r8-') ||
88:    detection.startsWith('r9-') ||
89:    detection.startsWith('r11-') ||
90:    detection.startsWith('r12-') ||
91:    detection === 'auto-label' ||
92:    detection === 'alt-click' ||
93:    detection === 'manual' ||
94:    detection === 'legacy' ||
95:    detection === 'custom-element' ||
96:    detection === 'cursor-dwell' ||
97:    detection === 'button-cluster' ||
98:    detection === 'hero' ||
99:    kind === 'click' ||
100:    kind === 'watch'
101:  ) return 'clickable';
102:
103:  return 'default';
104:}

item.text:
{"verdict":"warn","score":81,"summary":"The leaf-tag audit is a solid way to validate group routing and some hard semantic distinctions, but the current implementation overstates how independent and how precise the pattern-level number is. The main issues are an anchor detector that matches ancestor anchors instead of the leaf, an unconditional `input -> form` override that can hide button-like inputs, a pattern metric that is labeled as \"within correct group\" but currently includes wrong-group picks, and a truth function that reuses the same pill heuristic as the scorer, making the 90% closer to a compatibility score than an orthon",
"issues":[{"severity":"medium","category":"correctness","location":"services/canvas-engine/src/pattern-library/util.ts:17-19","description":"`isAnchorSelector()` matches any `> a` anywhere in the selector path, so a selector like `... > a > span` gets the anchor bump even though the region leaf is `span`. It also misses a selector that starts with `a` and has no preceding `>`. Because `clickable/link` adds `+35` from this helper, descendant nodes inside anchors can be over-scored as links for the wrong reason.","suggested_fix":"Make this a leaf-tag check instead of an ancestor check. Export a shared `leafTag(selector)` helper and use `return leafTag(selector) === 'a'`; if you keep regex-only logic, anchor it to the final segment rather than any `> a` occurrence."},{"severity":"medium","category":"correctness","location":"services/canvas-engine/src/pattern-library/normalize.ts:38-53","description":"The unconditional `input: 'form'` override collapses every `<input>` into the `form` group before pattern scoring. That includes submit/button/radio/checkbox inputs, which are semantically interactive controls rather than form-layout containers. The trainer has the same assumption in `TAG_TO_GROUP`, so this failure mode becomes self-confirming and will not show up in the reported accuracy.","suggested_fix":"Keep unconditional tag overrides for `textarea`, `select`, `ul`, and `ol`, but do not hard-route every `input`. Either leave `input` on detection-based routing, or gate the override to entry-like signals only (for example search/email/text naming or form-specific detections) so button-like inputs can still land in `clickable`."},{"severity":"medium","category":"correctness","location":"services/canvas-engine/test/trainer-vs-truth.mjs:183-189","description":"The script labels pattern accuracy as `sub-pattern within correct group`, but it computes `patternOk` for every decidable region regardless of whether `groupOk` was true. If group routing regresses later, the pattern metric will blend group and sub-pattern failures and stop measuring what its heading claims.","suggested_fix":"Only accumulate pattern-level stats after a correct group pick, e.g. `if (!groupOk) continue;` before `acceptablePatterns(...)`, or emit two separate numbers: one unconditional compatibility score and one true within-group sub-pattern score."},{"severity":"medium","category":"maintainability","location":"services/canvas-engine/test/trainer-vs-truth.mjs:54-56,77-81; services/canvas-engine/src/pattern-library/patterns/clickable-pill.ts:13-16","description":"The claimed \"independent truth\" for clickable sub-patterns is not fully independent: `acceptablePatterns()` reuses the same compact-plus-pill-text heuristic as the scorer, including the permissive `^\\S+$` branch. That means a compact one-word label like `Documentation` can be counted as an acceptable pill both in truth and in scoring, inflating the reported 90% while hiding overfitting to the same","suggested_fix":"Treat tag-driven semantics and stylistic compatibility as separate metrics. For the truth set, keep singleton semantic answers where the tag really constrains the result (`a -> link`, `button -> button`, plus clearly independent exceptions like true icon-sized controls), and report pill/cta flexibility as a secondary compatibility metric. If you keep the pill heuristic in scoring, remove the un"}],"strengths":["Using browser-emitted selector tags as a check against producer-side detection flattening is a good direction for group-level auditing.","The list/form tag overrides are simple and explainable for `textarea`, `select`, `ul`, and `ol`; the idea is cleaner than piling more detection literals into the normalizer.","The trainer’s output is actionable: mistake aggregation by `<tag> -> picked pattern` is the right shape for tuning the scorer without staring at raw captures."]}
